title: NVIDIA CUDA & GPU Programming Simplified
subtitle: How GPUs actually work and how to write code that uses them — CUDA, memory hierarchy, kernels, and the AI workloads driving the GPU era
description: A practical course on GPU programming through NVIDIA's CUDA platform. Covers the hardware mental model (what a GPU actually is and how it differs from a CPU), CUDA programming basics (kernels, threads, blocks, grids), the memory hierarchy that determines performance, optimization patterns, the CUDA ecosystem (cuBLAS, cuDNN, etc.), and the AI/ML workloads that drive the modern GPU economy. Built from NVIDIA's published documentation, developer blogs, and standard GPU programming references. Practical, code-aware, and grounded in what actually makes GPU programs fast.
audience: Developers, engineers, ML practitioners, and curious technical learners who want to actually understand GPUs. Some C/C++ familiarity helps but isn't required for the conceptual lessons.
prerequisites: Some C/C++ exposure helps but is not strictly required
outcomes: Explain what a GPU is and how it differs from a CPU at the hardware level
Write a basic CUDA kernel and understand the threading model
Map the CUDA memory hierarchy and choose the right memory for each access pattern
Diagnose and improve GPU performance bottlenecks
Use the CUDA ecosystem (cuBLAS, cuDNN, Thrust, etc.) effectively
Understand why AI/ML workloads dominate modern GPU usage
Evaluate when GPU acceleration is the right tool
